channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police deny sitting on evidence as Netflix doc brings renewed attention to JonBenet Ramsey's killing
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Amid renewed interest in the killing of JonBenet Ramsey triggered in part by a new Netflix documentary, police in Boulder, Colorado, refuted assertions this week that there is viable evidence and leads about the 1996 killing of the 6-year-old girl that they are not pursuing.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Child's toes 'severed' by lawnmower at Utah daycare; state wants it to shut down
item.description: A childcare center in southern Utah is losing its license and must cease operations by Dec. 4 after a child's toes were "severed" by a lawnmower.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs denied bail for a third time
item.description: He was denied bail in September and earlier this month when the judge deemed Combs a flight risk because of his money and connections.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, ABC 4

item.title: 2 injured after plane crashes into tree during emergency landing in California
item.description: Two people were injured Monday afternoon after a plane was forced to make an emergency landing and struck a tree.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Boulder Police address JonBenét Ramsey's case after Netflix docuseries premieres
item.description: Without specifically addressing the docuseries, Boulder Police called many of the claims in the show "completely false.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, KTLA 5

item.title: 3 killed, 1 seriously injured in Tesla Cybertruck crash in Northern California
item.description: PIEDMONT, Calif. (AP) — Three people were killed and one seriously injured early Wednesday when a Tesla Cybertruck crashed and caught fire in Northern California, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Diddy denied bail in sex trafficking, racketeering case
item.description: Sean "Diddy" Combs, the Bad Boy Records founder and billionaire hip-hop icon, has been denied bail in his federal sex trafficking and racketeering case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Birmingham nightclub massacre suspect accused of killing others in four separate shootings: police
item.description: The Alabama suspect accused of opening fire outside a Birmingham nightclub in September is now suspected of killing others in a mass shooting in July, police say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bay Area airport among 10 worst for on-time Thanksgiving travel: report
item.description: Depending on what airport your friends or relatives are flying out of, you may want to show a little extra compassion this Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Oregon tribe has hunting and fishing rights restored under a long-sought court ruling
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — Drumming made the floor vibrate and singing filled the conference room of the Chinook Winds Casino Resort in Lincoln City, on the Oregon coast, as hundreds in tribal regalia danced in a circle.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Mexican drug cartels allegedly used crypto to launder money
item.description: A DEA investigation is probing more than $15.7 million in cryptocurrency transfers reportedly involving drug proceeds
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) - Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Utah daycare loses license after child's toes 'severed' by lawnmower
item.description: "During this incident, a child was seriously injured, multiple toes were severed, and the child required emergency surgery," the DHHS said.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, NewsNation

item.title: Federal court dismisses defamation lawsuit against Fox News for Jan. 6 conspiracy theory
item.description: A federal judge has dismissed a defamation lawsuit against Fox News brought by a former Marine and Trump supporter who claimed he was made a scapegoat by the network after the Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol insurrection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade in NYC: See the balloons inflate before the big day
item.description: The 98th annual Macy’s Thanksgiving Day Parade will take over Manhattan Thursday morning, lining the streets with extravagant balloons and fanfare.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, KRON4

item.title: White supremacist prison gang leader accused of attacking two California prison officers
item.description: A white supremacist prison gang leader is accused in the attempted homicide of two officers at the California State Prison in Sacramento.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Docs: Ex-Avon choir teacher filmed sexual videos in his classroom, sent them to girls on Snapchat
item.description: AVON, Ind. — A former choir and theater teacher in the Avon school system faces felony charges after allegedly filming himself masturbating in his classroom and sending the videos to teen girls. Kayvon H.
item.dc:creator: Joe Schroeder, NewsNation

item.title: Residents file lawsuit against Kentucky plant that exploded in neighborhood
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Two neighbors of a Kentucky plant that was the site of a massive explosion are suing the company, saying the blast earlier this month damaged their homes and caused emotional trauma.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN and BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge finds New York City in contempt over jail conditions, moves closer to a federal takeover
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge on Wednesday found New York City in contempt for failing to staunch violence and brutality at its jails, a scathing ruling that puts the troubled Rikers Island jail complex on the verge of a federal takeover. In a written decision, U.S.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, MICHAEL R. SISAK and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is Blackout Wednesday: The unofficial holiday tradition you didn't know about
item.description: The idea of having a couple of drinks before the Thanksgiving chaos begins is so appealing to Americans that it has become an unofficial holiday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: PHOTOS: More than $18M in fake Gibson guitars seized by U.S. Customs and Border Protection
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection seized more than 3,000 fake Gibson guitars worth nearly $19 million, had they been genuine.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California man arrested for allegedly smuggling meth-caked clothing through LAX
item.description: Authorities found more than a dozen clothing items, including a cow pajama onesie, that were dried stiff and covered in a white residue.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, WGN 9

item.title: Lawmakers call for progress on marijuana law before end of Biden administration
item.description: Progressive lawmakers are calling on President Biden to take additional steps on federal marijuana law before his term comes to an end in January. In a letter sent to Biden and Vice President Harris, Democratic Reps. Barbara Lee (Calif.), Earl Blumenauer (Ore.) and Ilhan Omar (Minn.) and Sens.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Disney agrees to $43 million settlement in gender pay lawsuit
item.description: The agreement settles allegations initially made in a 2019 lawsuit filed by Disney employee LaRonda Rasmussen, who claimed she was getting paid less than her male counterparts in the same role.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: Texas builds border wall at ranch it wants to gift Trump for deportations
item.description: STARR COUNTY, Texas (Border Report) -- Under gray skies with patriotic music blaring in the background, construction crews on Tuesday put up a border wall on land the state recently bought and offered to the incoming Trump administration to use for deportation facilities.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Schools are bracing for upheaval over fear of mass deportations
item.description: Last time Donald Trump was president, rumors of immigration raids terrorized the Oregon community where Gustavo Balderas was the school superintendent. Word spread that immigration agents were going to try to enter schools.
item.dc:creator: BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 Americans held for years in China have been released, White House says
item.description: Three American citizens imprisoned for years by China have been released and are returning to the United States, the White House said Wednesday, announcing a rare diplomatic agreement with Beijing in the final months of the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured after more than 20 years on run
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Border Patrol trains more chaplains as the job and polarizing immigration debate rattle agents
item.description: DANIA BEACH, Florida (AP) — As immigration remains a hotly contested priority for the Trump administration after playing a decisive role in the deeply polarized election, the Border Patrol agents tasked with enforcing many of its laws are wrestling with growing challenges on and off the job.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zach Bryan pauses concert, calls out fan for throwing object on stage
item.description: A country music star whose 'argumentative' behavior has got him in hot water before, paused a recent concert after a fan appears to have thrown something onto stage.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KRON4

item.title: Most expensive residential sale in Tennessee history closes at $42.5M
item.description: Tennessee's most expensive residential real estate sale just happened in Williamson county. Twin Rivers Farm and its more than 383 acres is now off the market after an unnamed buyer snagged it for $42.5 million.
item.dc:creator: Nickelle Smith, NewsNation
